hypothesis: If machine learning interatomic potentials (EquiformerV2 and eSEN) are used to pre-converge solid-state nudged elastic band pathways before DFT refinement, then the hybrid ML-SSNEB framework will significantly reduce the total number of expensive DFT iterations required to locate minimum energy pathways and transition states compared to pure DFT-based SSNEB, because the ML models provide sufficiently accurate initial pathway geometries that place the DFT restart closer to convergence.
